channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Federal agents, hazmat sent to home of Ohio Secretary of State
item.description: Multiple agencies were sent to Ohio Secretary of State Frank Larose's residence for a reported "security incident.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, NewsNation

item.title: Americans end 2024 with grim economic outlook, but Republicans are optimistic for 2025: AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The unemployment rate is healthy and the stock market is up, but Democrats are feeling more pessimistic about the U.S. economy after Donald Trump's election victory, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jill Biden says she has closed the book on teaching at Northern Virginia Community College
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Jill Biden has closed the book on her teaching career at a Virginia community college. The first lady, who has spent the past 40 years teaching in classrooms, announced Monday that she had taught her final class at Northern Virginia Community College in Alexandria last week.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Opposition growing in Boston over plan to renovate historic stadium for women's professional soccer
item.description: Boston is planning to renovate a crumbling stadium in one of the city's most diverse neighborhood for its new professional women's soccer team.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DOGE Caucus founding member debuts 2 bills to kick-start waste cuts in Trump term
item.description: Sen. James Lankford, R-Okla., is introducing two bills targeting pandemic-era fraud as part of his advocacy for government reform with the Senate's DOGE Caucus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: After learning hard lessons in Austin, Greg Casar to take Congressional Progressive Caucus in new direction
item.description: The freshman congressman will lead the influential progressive caucus next year with plans to focus on working class and economic issues instead of culture wars.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Matthew Choi, KPRC 2

item.title: Americans' trust in nation's court system hits record low, survey finds
item.description: At a time of heightened political division, Americans’ confidence in their country’s judicial system and courts dropped to a record low of 35% this year.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Freedom Caucus reelects Andy Harris as chair
item.description: Rep. Andy Harris (R-Md.) will chair the House Freedom Caucus through the end of 2025, members of the group decided on Monday night, cementing his leadership of the hardline conservative caucus.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrats push plan to abolish Electoral College
item.description: Three Democratic senators unveiled a constitutional amendment to abolish the Electoral College system Monday, just more than a month after President-elect Trump stunned the Democrats by sweeping all seven battleground states, knocking off three Senate Democratic incumbents in the process. Sens.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: State lawsuit challenging Huntington Beach Voter ID law dismissed
item.description: A lawsuit filed by the state of California claiming Huntington Beach’s voter-approved voting ID measure “unlawfully conflicts with and is preempted by state law” has been dismissed, according to the city.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Jersey drones not national security risk: White House
item.description: Kirby stressed that “there are lots of drones in the sky” and the “vast majority of them are doing good things.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok asks the Supreme Court for an emergency order to block a U.S. ban unless it’s sold
item.description: A similar plea was filed by content creators who rely on the platform for income and some of TikTok’s more than 170 million users in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Lawmakers signal movement toward government funding deal
item.description: Top Republicans are signaling progress in government funding talks as leaders look to clinch a deal ahead of a looming Friday deadline. House Appropriations Chair Tom Cole (R-Okla.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden establishes a national monument for Frances Perkins, the 1st female Cabinet secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday established a national monument honoring the late FDR-era Labor Secretary Frances Perkins, the first woman to serve in a presidential Cabinet and a driving force behind Franklin D. Roosevelt's New Deal programs after the Great Depression.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, AAMER MADHANI and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams denied public funds for 2025 reelection campaign: CFB
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams was denied public funds for his 2025 reelection campaign, the New York City Campaign Finance Board announced on Monday. The CFB cited the federal charges against Adams as one of the reasons why it denied him the money.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Speaker Johnson faces 'complicated' decision on crucial Rules Committee chair
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is barreling into a tricky — yet critical — decision over whom to appoint as the next chair of the House Rules Committee, a post that will set the tone for legislating and play a key role in keeping Republicans united in next year’s ultrathin majority.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Trial over Minnesota House race that could swing control of chamber focuses on lost ballots
item.description: Republicans challenged the election result arguing that a 14-vote margin between the DFL representative who won and his GOP opponent could be made up in a pool of 20 ballots that were accidentally tossed.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Senator Schumer urges use of ROBIN to track mystery drones
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Sen. Chuck Schumer is making a plea for federal officials to launch a declassified special drone detection system called the ROBIN – a 360-degree technology across New York, New Jersey and the metro area to track any unidentified object in the sky.
item.dc:creator: Grace Gomez, PIX 11

item.title: Pelosi has hip replacement surgery at a US military hospital in Germany after a fall
item.description: Pelosi, 84, “is well on the mend,” said Ian Krager, a spokesman for the California Democrat, in a statement.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Manchin: Biden 'went completely insane on me' over American Rescue Plan opposition
item.description: “I was going to kill the whole bill. Then the president went completely insane on me,” Manchin said in an interview with news outlet Semafor that was published on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: DFL, Republican senators weigh in on state budget forecast
item.description: The state’s latest economic forecast, revealed Dec. 4, predicts less revenue than expected and higher-than-expected costs for some government programs. MPR News host Emily Bright spoke with state Sens.
item.dc:creator: Emily Bright and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Trump's picks for key positions in his second administration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has filled the key posts for his second term in office, prioritizing loyalty to him after he felt bruised and hampered by internal squabbling during his first term.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump trolls Trudeau as 'governor' of Canada
item.description: President-elect Trump mocked Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, calling him the "governor" of the "Great State of Canada.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5
